title: clematis integrifolia var. normalis
intro: Clematis integrifolia var. normalis is a perennial vine native to Eastern Australia and New South Wales, known for its fragrant, star-shaped flowers and edible parts. The plant's leaves and stems have been traditionally used in medicine, exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The leaves of Clematis integrifolia var. normalis have been used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, while also providing relief from pain and inflammation. The plant can be prepared as a tea or tincture, or used fresh in salads, with its leaves, flowers, and stems providing a variety of health benefits.
